description: Cognitive-Resonant IMRaD: A Unified Standard for High-Fidelity Knowledge Distillation
author: Jesse Haskin / DodecaGone Systems
changelog: 3.1.1: Separated Page Reference Block (citations) from Footer (boilerplate). Citations now live in a horizontal-rule-delimited zone at bottom of content area, NOT in the document footer. Footer is static boilerplate only.
3.1.1: Header alignment corrected to right-aligned with pipe-delimited authors and page number.
3.1.1: Renamed Simple Summary to Simplest Summary throughout for consistency with master doc.
3.1.1: Format constraint tightened to paragraph only (no bullets in Simplest Summary).
3.1.1: Weasel word flagging moved from AMBER to GREEN (deterministic detection).
3.1.1: Added V28-V30 validators for Page Reference Block, footer boilerplate, and horizontal rule presence.
3.1.1: Added relational concept rule to pagination (Section 8.0 of master doc).

excluded_domains.reason: Emergency text requires somatic ultra-brief directives. Hourglass narrative introduces unacceptable cognitive load during amygdala hijack.
Mathematical structures rely on compressed topological notation. Plain-language constraints degrade structural integrity.
Brevity, objectivity, and anti-passive-voice rules conflict with aesthetic and emotional goals of literature.

rules: Each page must function as a verifiable robust link in a conceptual chain contributing a single discrete unit of knowledge
Complex arguments may span multiple pages but must maintain clear conceptual continuity
No widowed headings
No orphan sentences
If a concept is relational define it by its connections not by being a box itself

header.example: Author A | Author B | Author C

page_reference_block._description: The Zero-Flipping Zone. This is NOT the document footer. It is a distinct unpaginated section at the bottom of the main content area, separated from body text by a thin horizontal rule.
page_reference_block.example:
1 Sweller, J. (2024). Cognitive Load Theory. This study establishes the intrinsic load limit for technical instruction.
2 Redish, J. (2023). Letting go of the perfect: Writing for comprehension.
page_reference_block.rule: Citations must exist on the same page as their reference. Zero page-flipping. Each citation on its own line, NOT pipe-delimited.

footer._description: Static boilerplate only. Citations have moved to the Page Reference Block above.
footer.rule: Footer content is identical on every page. No per-page variation. No citations.

sections.name: introduction
rules: You may NOT introduce a technical concept using technical terms
You MUST begin with an accessible universally understood physical metaphor
Leverages elaborative rehearsal: connecting new information to familiar experiences

sections.name: methods
rules: The narrow neck of the hourglass
Map the metaphor to scientific reality with explicit mathematically defensible correspondences

sections.name: results_and_discussion
rules: Resolve the metaphor problem using the technical truth
Reader should finish feeling satiated by data density or questions posed

requirements: Most accessible section of entire document
Accompanied by a summarizing visual aid centered on page 0 if able with descriptive text immediately below
simplest_summary.automation_note: LLM can draft. Author MUST review and confirm.

rules: Replace 'to be' verbs with strong action verbs
Remove weasel words: slightly, mostly, seems, somewhat, arguably
Be extremely clear and concise

simple_concept_test.a_or_an_test: Every defined concept must be expressible as 'a [noun]' or 'an [noun]'
simple_concept_test.relationship_test: Relationships between concepts must form valid readable English sentences
simple_concept_test.action_mandate: If a concept cannot pass these tests it must be rewritten

rules: Bold 1-3 critical concepts or keywords per paragraph
Bolded words must form a grammatically sensible summary when read in sequence
Do NOT bold non-essential words
The skimmable path IS the author's thesis compressed into visual scaffold
skimmable_path.automation_note: Machine can suggest speculative bolding based on term frequency and position. Author MUST confirm. Final bold = act of authorial intent.

in_text.rule: Keep skimmable path free of citation clutter

citation_mode.description: Two modes available. Author selects based on document type.

explanatory.use_case: Primers, manuals, CR-IMRaD conversions of external research
explanatory.reference_block_format: Author Name (Year). Title of Work. (One-sentence plain-language takeaway).
explanatory.example: 1 Sweller, J. (2024). Cognitive Load Theory. This study establishes the intrinsic load limit for technical instruction.

compact.use_case: Formal frameworks, theorems, technical specifications, self-citing documents
compact.reference_block_format: Page/Section ranges, Author Name, Title (Year). [persistent link]
compact.example: 1  1-26, 63, 99-100, Haskin J., YOU ARE HERE: Keep Existing (2026). dodecagone.net

visual_integration.rule: If a source supports a diagram citation must be integrated into the descriptive caption not relegated to a separate list

citations.simple_concept_test: Every citation must be identifiable as 'a specific study' or 'an expert voice'

knowledge_aids.definition: Any non-prose visual element intended to accelerate comprehension. Includes diagrams, flowcharts, cartoons, infographics, tables, glyphs, annotated photographs, spatial metaphor maps. Excludes decorative images, stock photography, elements with no informational load.
knowledge_aids.removal_test: If removing the visual would not reduce the reader's comprehension it does not belong in the document.

figure_number.rule: X = section number, Y = sequence within section

descriptive_caption.content: Must describe what reader is seeing AND what conclusion to draw

source_attribution.rule: Integrated into caption per Citation Standard. If original state: 'Original.'

alt_text.rule: For digital output every visual must include screen-reader-accessible alt text conveying equivalent informational content
alt_text.principle: A blind reader must receive equivalent comprehension

quality_gates.replacement_test: Could this visual be replaced by a single sentence without loss of comprehension? If yes use the sentence.
quality_gates.standalone_test: Can a reader understand the visual core message from figure caption and labels alone without reading surrounding body text? If no the visual is incomplete.

spatial_integration.proximity: Visual must appear within one page of first textual reference

orientation_matching.vertical_diagram: Two-column layout or vertical text block adjacent. Prevents long eye-travel distances.
orientation_matching.horizontal_diagram: Full-width blocks above and below. Maintains natural downward reading flow.
orientation_matching.complex_circular: Callout lines from text to image parts. Eliminates need for separate legend.

spatial_integration.no_orphaned_figures: A knowledge aid must never appear on a page by itself with no accompanying text
spatial_integration.flow_preservation: Reader eye should never jump backward or laterally more than one column width to resume reading

cartoons.narrative_function: Must map directly to a technical concept. Visual form of the Universal Metaphor not decoration.
cartoons.humor: Permitted and encouraged when it serves comprehension. The joke serves the truth; the truth does not serve the joke.
cartoons.recurring_visual_language: Establish consistent visual vocabulary and maintain throughout. Do not introduce new visual language on every page.

tables.row_headers: Clearly distinguished using weight or shading contrast

acknowledgements.definition: Recognizes entities that contributed but do not meet authorship threshold
acknowledgements.authorship_threshold: Substantive intellectual contributions to content structure or conclusions

categories.format: Name provided [specific environmental or support function]

constraints: Every acknowledgement must pass Simple Concept Test

categories.if_none: This work received no direct funding.

categories.examples: Platform subscriptions, hardware access, API credits, donated services

categories.if_none: The authors declare no conflicts of interest.

funding_disclosures.fidelity_rule: Omission of a material funding source constitutes a fidelity violation

affiliations.anti_inflation: Affiliation must accurately reflect context of production not aspirational context
affiliations.ai_contributors: Listed in Acknowledgements not Affiliations unless authorship framework explicitly defines AI as co-author. If co-author: platform and version as affiliation.

rules: One action per sentence
Use vertical lists for multi-step procedures
Begin safety instructions with a command or condition
Each prerequisite procedure exists as single standalone module in CMS or DITA/XML
Writers insert programmatic callout
Publishing platform renders prerequisite steps in place
Users never navigate away
Writers maintain only one master version

typography.font_selection: Based on legibility for output medium not family. Both serif and sans-serif acceptable. Accessibility and comfort of reading are paramount.

contrast_requirements.rule: Size and weight contrast to distinguish headings from body

typography.negative_space: Generous white space reduces intimidation and improves readability

visual_pov.zoom_rule: Zoom for detail but never so far that context is lost

rules: Reading level equals writing level
Hand draft to a novice
Every 'I don't know what this means' requires revision or consideration
Spell out acronyms on first use
Provide inline definitions or glossary for unavoidable technical terms
Embed feedback mechanisms in digital modules

multimedia.descriptive_captions: Every visual element must include descriptive caption placed directly adjacent
multimedia.audio_narration: Audio buttons leverage modality effect: simultaneous auditory + visual channels reduce cognitive load

gifs_short_video.rule: Targeted clips to demonstrate motion or change

GREEN.description: Fully automatable. Machine applies with confidence. No author review needed.
examples: Page Reference Block horizontal rule and formatting
Citation placement in Page Reference Block

AMBER.description: Machine suggests. Author must review and confirm.

RED.description: Cannot automate. Author must write or decide.
examples: Humor judgment in cartoons (whether joke serves truth)

validator_checks.rule: Paragraph contains more than one topic
Citation not on same page as reference
Citation missing summary (Mode A) or persistent link (Mode B)
Figure appears more than one page from first textual reference
Orphaned figure on page with no accompanying text
No bolding detected in paragraph (missing skimmable path)
More than 3 bold segments in single paragraph
Simplest Summary has fewer than 3 sentences
Introduction opens with technical terms before metaphor
Widowed heading at bottom of page
Cartoon panel contains more than 3 concepts
Page contains citations but no Page Reference Block with horizontal rule separator
Footer contains per-page variable content (should be static boilerplate only)
Page Reference Block uses pipe delimiters instead of line breaks between citations
